evals.prompt: I want to start a fresh C++ project that uses Dear ImGui. Target: a basic tools window I can hack on, with the ImGui demo window visible so I can browse what's available. I'm on Linux, I have CMake and clang installed. Just get me to a working hello-world I can build and run.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded project with CMakeLists.txt (FetchContent for imgui v1.92.x docking + GLFW), src/main.cpp (with NewFrame/Render lifecycle, ShowDemoWindow, modern conventions), and clear build/run instructions. RAII guards used in any sample code.

Identifies the issue as ImGui ID stack collision: every Button("Delete") in the loop has the same label and same parent ID, so ImGui treats them as one button and reports clicks against the FIRST one. Fix: wrap each iteration in PushID/PopID (preferably via an RAII guard like ImScoped::ID{&tab} or {i}). Should mention the ## or ### suffix as alternative.

Identifies the AutoResizeY + content-determines-size feedback loop: AutoResizeY makes the child grow to fit content, but text wrapping inside the child depends on the available width which depends on the child's size. Fix recommendations: (a) pin a fixed height via ImVec2(0, k_height); (b) use SetNextWindowSizeConstraints to bound growth; (c) use ImGuiChildFlags_ResizeY to give the user control. Should reference layout-and-sizing.md for deeper detail.
